description: Legal document. A physician Shemuel grants a loan of 400 cut-up dirhams to Abū l-Faraj the broker (simsār) in Fustat and Cairo, to be paid back at the end of Tishri 1537 = end of Ramaḍān and beginning of Shawwāl 622 AH = October 1225 CE. Unfortunately for Shemuel, cut up dirhams would be taken out of circulation one month after the loan was due to be repaid. The deed also states that Shemuel had previously sold Abu l-Faraj sugar and sugar molasses. Signed by ʿAmram b. Ḥalfon ha-Levi and Shelomo b. Natan ha-Levi. Possibly in the hand of Shelomo b. Eliyyahu. (Information from CUDL, Goitein's index card, and Mediterranean Society, I, 385.)
description_he: מסמך משפטי. הרופא שמואל נותן הלוואה של 400 דרהם חתוכים לאבו אל-פרג' הסרסור בפוסטאט ובקהיר, להחזר בתשלומים חודשיים של 2 דינרים. מתוארך כסלו 566 היג'ר = נובמבר 1170.
